extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint won the 14km Stage 1 time trial by pacing the final 5km perfectly, taking 12 seconds from MARTIN Tony in the last 3 miles.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint gained 12 seconds in final 5km through superior pacing strategy, not early aggression—late acceleration decisive.
Team Sky's equipment choices (tire width, pressure, aerodynamics) created marginal gains that accumulated to multi-second advantages over 14km.
PORTE Richie's conservative cornering strategy cost 35 seconds to FROOME Chris despite going flat-out on straights—cautious tactics backfired.
MARTIN Tony faded in final kilometers under home-race pressure, suggesting psychological load affected his pacing discipline.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: First TT victory shows GC capability beyond domestique role, pacing discipline exceptional in final kilometers
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Only 12 seconds behind while reportedly not fully focused on TT, 35-second gap to Porte sends leadership message
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: 47 seconds down reveals critical weakness—cautious cornering didn't compensate for straightaway power deficit versus FROOME
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: negative: Home pressure and suboptimal pacing caused fade in final 5km, lost 12 seconds to THOMAS in closing stretch
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: Second place at 5 seconds as young rider marks him as future TT specialist with exceptional power
